Psoriatic arthritis 
Psoriatic arthritis is a long-term condition that causes joint pain, swelling and stiffness. Medicines can help treat the symptoms.
Some people with the skin condition psoriasis will get psoriatic arthritis as they get older.
Symptoms of psoriatic arthritis 
Symptoms of psoriatic arthritis include:
pain, swelling and stiffness in 1 or more joints swollen fingers or toes your nails changing from their usual colour, or tiny dents or pits developing in your nails feeling drained of energy Symptoms can be worse first thing in the morning and may improve with exercise.
Some people may also get symptoms of psoriasis , such as dry scaly skin, at the same time.
Psoriatic arthritis can cause swelling of the fingers
